item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-014","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer rejects the compressed binary by introducing the third state of modular monolith, then gives structural coordinates around team size, stable domain boundaries, independent release/scaling/fault isolation, and DevOps/observability maturity. It commits clearly to modular monolith for new/early/small-team/changing-business contexts and gives a trigger for later microservice extraction, so it avoids both direct binary simplification and vague 'it depends' mush."}
